methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Diode is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Davide Asnaghi and Lenny Khazan, having raised $10M+. The target operates within a sector represented by 1 public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ) and 8 private peers including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable valuation approach. Using total capital raised of $10M as a proxy baseline and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage progression, this method implies a $25M valuation. Peer-median triangulation is not applicable given insufficient narrow-stage comparables within robotics and manufacturing; while 7 same-sector peers exist, none satisfy stricter filters for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier valuations. Applying an all-sector median would lack sufficient specificity to this company's profile.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $21M to $29M, with a median estimate of $25M. This range reflects a ±15% sensitivity band around the stage-progression point estimate. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct same-stage peer data—which would strengthen confidence in the range—and reliance on a single applicable method. Any subsequent round terms, revenue trajectory, or capital intensity metrics would materially inform this estimate.
